metaDescription: New to Microsoft Loop? Learn how to sign in, build your first workspace, add pages, and share live components with Teams and Outlook.
intro: If you've never used Microsoft Loop before, the combination of workspaces, pages, and portable components can feel unfamiliar at first. This beginner-friendly guide walks through signing in, creating your first workspace and page, and sharing a live component into another Microsoft 365 app.

steps.heading: Sign in to Loop
steps.body: Open loop.cloud.microsoft in your browser and sign in to authenticate your Microsoft account.

steps.heading: Create your first workspace
steps.body: Click the plus icon to launch the workspace creation drawer, then enter a project title and assign a style icon.

steps.heading: Add your first page
steps.body: Go to the left sidebar and click Add New Page to open a fresh editing canvas inside your workspace.

steps.heading: Use slash commands to build content
steps.body: Place your cursor on a free line and type / to reveal the full list of available components, like tables and task lists.

steps.heading: Drop in a component and tag a stakeholder
steps.body: Select a task list or table, add content, and tag a teammate so background webhooks keep the data synced.

steps.heading: Share a live component elsewhere
steps.body: Open the block header options, choose Create Component, and share the resulting URL into a Teams chat for others to edit in real time.

faq.question: Do I need a specific Microsoft 365 plan to use Loop?
faq.answer: Loop is part of the broader Microsoft 365 ecosystem, and access generally depends on the plan and licensing your organization has in place.

faq.question: What's the difference between a workspace and a page in Loop?
faq.answer: A workspace is a container that groups related pages together, while a page is an individual editable canvas inside that workspace.

faq.question: What is a Loop component exactly?
faq.answer: A component is a portable content block, like a table or task list, that stays live and editable even when shared outside the original page, such as in a Teams chat or email.

faq.question: Can I use Loop on mobile as a beginner?
faq.answer: Loop is designed to work across web, mobile, and desktop, so you can start a workspace on one device and continue on another.
